title: Cinder Procession
summary: On the night of Glassmere's Emberwake parade, route director Alina Rohe is found dead beneath a ceremonial arch in what the city calls a tragic setup accident. A missing blue folder, an unsigned note, and a sealed service gate suggest the body was staged and the parade was used as cover.

evidence.title: Opening Brief
evidence.summary: Ashfall's intake summary of the death at the Emberwake procession.

evidence.title: Festival Incident Report
evidence.summary: The official account of how Alina Rohe was found beneath the North Arch.

evidence.title: Emberwake Route Map
evidence.summary: A simplified procession map showing the review table, North Arch, and older service access points.

evidence.title: Pocket Note
evidence.summary: A note recovered from the victim that points to a specific meeting place before the first bell.

evidence.title: Marshal Checkpoints
evidence.summary: Checkpoint entries for route marshals working the parade perimeter.

evidence.title: Maintenance Key Log
evidence.summary: The checkout log for service keys used during festival setup.

evidence.title: Reroute Orders
evidence.summary: Last-minute staffing changes issued minutes before first bell.

evidence.title: Treasurer Re-Interview
evidence.summary: A second interview with the festival treasurer about the victim's missing blue folder.

evidence.title: Reimbursement Ledger
evidence.summary: Festival reimbursement entries showing repeated payments to the same storage vendor.

evidence.title: Supply Cart Search Report
evidence.summary: A field report describing what was recovered from a route cart assigned to the deputy director.

stages.title: Before First Bell
stages.summary: Figure out where the victim intended to go before the parade began.
handlerPrompts: Start with the unsigned note and translate it into a precise place.
The route diagram is there to turn vague language into a usable code.

objectives.prompt: Which gate code matches the meeting point in the victim's note?

stages.title: Private Window
stages.summary: Identify the two findings that prove somebody created a private access window at the gate.
handlerPrompts: Now that you have the gate, ask who could clear the area and still open it.
Compare key custody with staff movement. Opportunity is being built in plain sight.

objectives.prompt: Which two findings prove someone deliberately created a private meeting window at Gate C4?

options.label: Bram Sayer checked out the service master key before first bell.
The incident report says the North Arch pin failed after the body was found.
Post Three was reassigned away from Gate C4 minutes before the meeting.
The pocket note says the victim should bring the blue folder herself.

stages.title: The Blue Folder
stages.summary: Use the new financial and search evidence to decide who killed Alina Rohe.
handlerPrompts: Do not stop at access. Find the reason the victim brought that folder in person.
One suspect had motive, opportunity, and a recovered trace of the missing folder.

objectives.prompt: Who killed Alina Rohe and staged the North Arch accident?
